name: Asteroid Belt Sigma-9
notes: Dense asteroid field with predictable drift patterns. Rich in rare-earth minerals. Navigable with standard deflector arrays.

name: Europa
notes: Subsurface ocean confirmed. Hydrothermal vents detected at 14 km depth. Radiation from Jupiter requires shielded approach corridors.

name: Nebula Frontier Sector 7
notes: Emission nebula with extreme electromagnetic interference. Sensor range reduced to 40%. Contains uncharted gravitational anomalies.

name: Titan
notes: Dense atmosphere of nitrogen and methane. Subsurface ocean beneath ice crust. Viable candidate for permanent outpost.

name: Comet Helix-7
notes: Highly eccentric orbit. Volatile outgassing intensifies near perihelion. Approach only during calculated intercept windows.

name: Obsidian Station
notes: Decommissioned research outpost on Asteroid Kappa-12. Structural integrity compromised after hull breach incident. Reactor in standby mode.

name: Phantom Rift
notes: Anomalous dark matter concentration. Gravitational lensing distorts navigation systems. Two survey probes lost in this region. Extreme caution advised.

name: Kepler's Remnant
notes: Supernova remnant with intense radiation bursts. Contains rare heavy elements. Approach requires Class-V radiation shielding.

name: Veridia Prime
notes: Rocky terrestrial world with thin atmosphere. Stable geothermal activity. Strong candidate for forward exploration base.

name: Glacius
notes: Ice giant with extensive ring system. Deuterium-rich atmosphere makes it a potential fuel depot. Calm orbital environment.

name: Asteroid Theta-7
notes: Slow-rotating metallic asteroid. Ideal for automated mining operations. Close proximity to Nebula Forge makes regular supply runs feasible.

name: Nebula Forge Station
notes: Home base. Primary space station and command hub for all exploration operations in this sector.
